title: polypodium iridifolium
intro: Polypodium iridifolium, also known as Polypodium plebejum, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Polypodium iridifolium include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves, which are dried and then used to create teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
